issue: The system is throwing hybrid database connection errors stating that the ENVS lack Supabase API keys.

timeline.detail: Selecting candidate files based on issue keywords.
Selected 14 file(s): supabase/HYBRID_SETUP.md, admin/includes/hybrid_admin_read.php, hybrid_dual_write.php, admin/profile_settings.php, admin/settings.php, .env ...
Reading bounded snippets from candidate files.

result.summary: Supabase API keys missing in production environment
result.explanation: The system is configured for hybrid dual-write mode but cannot connect to Supabase because the required environment variables SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY are not set or are empty. This causes all Supabase operations to fail, preventing database writes and admin read functionality.
result.root_cause: Missing or empty Supabase configuration in production environment variables
checks: Verify SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY exist in .env
Test Supabase connection using the test functions in settings.php
Check file permissions on .env to ensure it's readable by the web server
fix_options: Add SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY to the production .env file with valid Supabase project credentials
Verify the Supabase service role key has not been rotated or invalidated
Ensure the .env file is properly loaded and accessible by the PHP application
tradeoffs: Adding production credentials requires secure handling to prevent exposure
Without Supabase connection, the system falls back to file-only mode which may not meet requirements
result.patch_preview: Add missing Supabase environment variables to .env file
result.improved_code: No code changes needed - configuration issue
notes: The system will continue to work in file-only mode if Supabase is unavailable
Consider implementing proper error handling for missing Supabase configuration
Ensure .env is not committed to version control in production
